title: The 10 Most Anticipated Movies of Summer 2023, and the Most Surprising Film of the Year So Far
format: Sean and Amanda count down their five most-anticipated movies of the summer, argue about when summer movie season actually starts, and Blackberry director Matt Johnson stops by to talk Glenn Howerton, long lenses, and Canada's marketing problem.

segments.heading: Blackberry
segments.note: the docudrama, before the director interview

films.title: Blackberry
films.note: Matt Johnson's docudrama on the rise and fall of BlackBerry, based on the book Losing the Signal. Jay Baruchel and Glenn Howerton play RIM's co-founders; Sean and Amanda single out Howerton's turn as a triumph of 'psycho capitalism.' Shot with long lenses to mimic a nature documentary watching its subjects from a wary distance.

films.title: Fast X
films.note: Sean calls it the true start of summer movie season. Vin Diesel says it's the first of a planned trilogy; reported $350 million budget.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: Referenced for its unusually strong second-weekend box office hold.

films.title: Book Club: The Next Chapter
films.note: Underperformed at the box office; joked that its older female audience defected to Guardians 3.

films.title: Hypnotic
films.note: Robert Rodriguez's new Ben Affleck thriller; reviews described as quite poor.

films.title: Transformers: Rise of the Beasts
films.note: Didn't make Sean's personal list, but he says he's 'locked and loaded' to see it.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One
films.note: No. 1 - Sean's first-ever piece for the show was an interview tied to the prior Mission: Impossible film; opens July 12.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: No. 2 - Discussed mostly through the fallout from Sean's own trailer commentary, which set off a minor Twitter pile-on. Opens July 21, the same day as Barbie.

films.title: The Boogeyman
films.note: No. 3 - Rob Savage (Host, Dashcam) directs; Chris Messina stars. Opens June 2.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny
films.note: No. 4 - James Mangold directs; Harrison Ford and Phoebe Waller-Bridge star. Opens June 30.

films.title: No Hard Feelings
films.note: No. 5 - Gene Stupnitsky's sex comedy with Jennifer Lawrence, hired by parents to 'date' their teenage son. Opens June 23.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: No. 1 - Greta Gerwig directs, co-written with Noah Baumbach; Margot Robbie and Ryan Gosling star. Opens July 21 opposite Oppenheimer.

films.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
films.note: No. 2 - Co-directed by Kemp Powers (Soul, One Night in Miami), who replaces Peter Ramsey from the first film. Oscar Isaac, Daniel Kaluuya, and Issa Rae voice cast.

films.title: Past Lives
films.note: No. 3 - Celine Song's A24 romantic drama with Greta Lee, Teo Yoo, and John Magaro; Sean predicts a Best Picture nomination. Opens June 2.

films.title: Bottoms
films.note: No. 4 - Emma Seligman (Shiva Baby) directs Rachel Sennott and Ayo Edebiri in a Sundance/SXSW breakout. Opens August 25.

films.title: Asteroid City
films.note: No. 5 - Wes Anderson's ensemble with Tom Hanks, Scarlett Johansson, and Jason Schwartzman. Opens June 16 in limited release.

films.title: Haunted Mansion
films.note: The other option for the birthday-weekend trip to the theater.

films.title: Talk to Me
films.note: A24 horror pickup Amanda already saw at Sundance; wants to catch it again on a big screen.

films.title: Blackberry
films.note: Johnson made the film for about $5.5-6 million, shot on long National Geographic-style lenses that pull farther back as the company's stakes rise.

films.title: The Dirties
films.note: Johnson's fake-documentary debut about a school shooter; his real mother appears as herself, unaware she was being filmed.

films.title: Operation Avalanche
films.note: Johnson's follow-up, about CIA operatives infiltrating NASA.

films.title: F for Fake
films.note: Orson Welles' film about fakery and magic; Johnson calls it his favorite movie and the root of his whole approach to filmmaking.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: Cited as an inevitable comparison to Blackberry; Johnson calls it one of his favorite films of the century and says Fincher's precision is the opposite of his own found, chaotic style.

films.title: Dungeons & Dragons: Honor Among Thieves
films.note: Johnson's answer for the last great thing he's seen - he says he cried at a Toronto sneak preview.

films.title: Grizzly Man
films.note: Referenced as a lesser version of the same effect Johnson chases in Crumb.

films.title: Crumb
films.note: Documentary Johnson calls a masterpiece and a huge influence on Blackberry.

films.title: The War Room
films.note: D.A. Pennebaker's Clinton-campaign documentary; the model for Blackberry's long lenses growing more distant as its subjects gain power.

films.title: Original Cast Album: Company
films.note: Pennebaker's documentary on the Sondheim musical's cast recording, cited alongside The War Room as an influence.

films.title: Capturing the Friedmans
films.note: Cited as a major documentary influence.

films.title: Safety Not Guaranteed
films.note: Referenced via Colin Trevorrow's path from indie film to studio tentpole.

films.title: Jurassic World
films.note: Trevorrow's studio follow-up, cited as the kind of leap Johnson isn't chasing.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Sean interviewed James Mangold in his office for this film.

films.title: Logan
films.note: Cited as proof Mangold can handle a big budget without losing the plot.

films.title: Goon
films.note: Jay Baruchel's directing credit, from his own prior appearance on the show.

films: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem

Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Par.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One

Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny

Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant May.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem
